title: quercus × rudkinii
intro: Quercus × rudkinii, also known as Rudkin's oak, is a hybrid plant resulting from the cross between Quercus robur and Quercus petraea. The medicinal properties of Quercus × rudkinii include its high tannin content, which has been traditionally used to treat diarrhea and other gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits associated with Quercus × rudkinii are mainly due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases. Herbal preparations of Quercus × rudkinii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
